agent_raw: Critique wins. The hospital-center backtest cannot justify mobile screening deployment for three reasons. First, verification-biased labels: ophthalmologist confirmation was available only for patients who completed specialty exams after referral, systematically excluding unconfirmed patients who may have had disease. Second, device and operator shift: mobile camera and nurse workflow differences create a prospective distribution shift not characterized by the backtest. Third, missing threshold-level analysis: the referral capacity claim requires sensitivity and referral volume at the operating threshold in the lower-prevalence mobile population, which the aggregate hospital metrics do not provide. The QC rule routing unreadable images to manual review is a sensible safeguard. Lower prevalence alone is not a disqualifier — it changes calibration needs. A prospective mobile pilot with unbiased labeling is the required next step.
